description_long: This object is a black, solid-body electric guitar with a classic single-cutaway design reminiscent of a Les Paul style. The body is outlined with a white trim, and the neck features a series of colored buttons instead of traditional frets, suggesting it is a guitar controller for a music video game. The fretboard is black with five prominent colored buttons—green, red, yellow, blue, and orange—arranged in a row. The headstock is simple and lacks visible tuning pegs or branding, further supporting the idea that this is a game controller rather than a functional instrument. The surface appears smooth and glossy, with minimal hardware or detailing beyond the colored buttons and a single white line on the body.
description: A black, single-cutaway electric guitar controller with colored buttons on the neck and a smooth, glossy finish. The body is outlined in white and lacks traditional guitar hardware.
description_view_0: Side view showing the guitar's black body, white outline, and the colored buttons on the neck.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the colored buttons on the neck and the rounded body shape.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the body contour, white trim, and button layout on the neck.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the body and neck, with the colored buttons visible at the far end.
attribute_headstock_design: Simple, rectangular headstock with no visible tuning pegs or logos
